inputs.label: Compressor Power
inputs.businessContext: Nominal power rating of the compressor motor. Based on ISO 1217 standard for compressor performance testing.

inputs.label: Load Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of time the compressor operates at full load. Typical values from compressed air system audits (ISO 11011).

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Day
inputs.businessContext: Daily operating hours. Based on shift patterns and production schedules.

inputs.label: Operating Days per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating days. Typical for industrial facilities.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost
inputs.businessContext: Average electricity price. Based on local utility rates or EIA data.

inputs.label: Motor Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Efficiency of the electric motor. Based on NEMA MG1 or IEC 60034 standards.

inputs.label: Compressor Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of compressor affects maintenance and efficiency factors. Based on ISO 5390 classification.

inputs.label: Maintenance Factor
inputs.businessContext: Additional energy loss due to poor maintenance (e.g., leaks, dirty filters). Based on DOE compressed air guidelines.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data accuracy. Used for risk-adjusted outputs.

thresholds.loadFactor < 50: Low load factor indicates oversized compressor or inefficient operation. Consider variable speed drive or system optimization.
thresholds.motorEfficiency < 85: Motor efficiency below standard. Consider replacing with high-efficiency motor (NEMA Premium or IE3).
thresholds.maintenanceFactor > 10: High maintenance factor suggests significant leaks or poor maintenance. Conduct leak detection and repair program.

suggestedActions: loadFactor < 50 ? 'Consider installing variable speed drive or staging multiple compressors.' : ''
motorEfficiency < 85 ? 'Replace motor with high-efficiency model (NEMA Premium or IE3).' : ''
maintenanceFactor > 10 ? 'Conduct compressed air leak detection and repair program.' : ''
